title: I Am The Way
content:
Thou art the Way.
    Hadst Thou been nothing but the goal,
         I cannot say
    If Thou hadst ever met my soul.

         I cannot see--
    I, child of process--if there lies
         An end for me,
    Full of repose, full of replies.

         I'll not reproach
    The way that goes, my feet that stir.
         Access, approach,
    Art Thou, time, way, and wayfarer.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alice Christiana Thompson Meynell, titled "I Am The Way", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
